intent: Write a 3-source report on 'pet nutrition basics for dog owners' — storefront inventory, community wisdom, and Wikipedia-backed fundamentals. (1) One Stop Market shopping site — browse the pet-supplies category and collect at least 4 dog-food-relevant products (dry food, wet food, supplement, treat). Record name, price, rating, review count. (2) Reddit — browse /f/AskReddit AND /f/science for posts discussing dog nutrition, brand skepticism, or supplement evidence. Collect at least 3 posts with title, score, comment count. (3) Wikipedia — open 'Dog food' or 'Canine nutrition' (whichever is available) and extract 3-4 facts about AAFCO standards or nutrient requirements. The report must: (a) present the 4 storefront picks with stats, (b) reconcile any storefront marketing claim with Wikipedia nutrition facts, (c) integrate community skepticism about grain-free, raw, or premium marketing, (d) close with a buyer's checklist. Cite every claim.
author_notes: Hard 3-site. Dog nutrition with science grounding.
